interfaces.name: Event
interfaces.signature: struct Event: Identifiable, Codable, Sendable { let id: UUID; var title: String; var cues: [Cue]; var createdAt: Date; var schemaVersion: Int }
interfaces.notes: Lives in app target's Core/ folder, not a package. One JSON file per event. cues order is authoritative sequence order.

interfaces.name: Cue
interfaces.signature: struct Cue: Identifiable, Codable, Sendable, Equatable { let id: UUID; var title: String; var duration: TimeInterval; var role: String?; var contingencyNote: String?; var backupTargetCueID: UUID? }
interfaces.notes: duration == 0 is a valid 'instant' cue. backupTargetCueID references another Cue.id, never an index.

interfaces.name: ActionLogEntry
interfaces.signature: enum ActionLogEntry: Codable, Sendable { case start(at: Date, uptime: TimeInterval); case advance(at: Date, uptime: TimeInterval, toCueID: UUID); case skip(at: Date, uptime: TimeInterval, toCueID: UUID); case holdStarted(at: Date, uptime: TimeInterval); case holdEnded(at: Date, uptime: TimeInterval); case jumpToBackup(at: Date, uptime: TimeInterval, toCueID: UUID); case undoLastAction(at: Date, uptime: TimeInterval); case complete(at: Date, uptime: TimeInterval) }
interfaces.notes: Append-only. Every entry carries both wall-clock and monotonic uptime so LiveRunEngine can do clock-jump detection without a separate log.

interfaces.name: EventLog
interfaces.signature: struct EventLog: Codable, Sendable { let eventID: UUID; var entries: [ActionLogEntry] }
interfaces.notes: One JSON file per event, alongside the Event file. Never truncated, even by undo.

interfaces.name: LiveRunProjection
interfaces.signature: struct LiveRunProjection: Sendable, Equatable { var currentCueID: UUID?; var isLive: Bool; var isOnHold: Bool; var isComplete: Bool; var elapsedInCurrentCue: TimeInterval; var driftFromPlanned: TimeInterval; var completedCueIDs: [UUID]; var skippedCueIDs: [UUID]; var canUndo: Bool; var clockJumpWarning: Bool }
interfaces.notes: The ONLY type LiveConsoleView's body should read. Equatable so SwiftUI can skip re-renders when nothing meaningful changed.

interfaces.name: ClockProviding
interfaces.signature: protocol ClockProviding: Sendable { func now() -> Date; func uptime() -> TimeInterval }
interfaces.notes: Injected everywhere; no direct Date()/ProcessInfo calls inside LiveRunEngine or view models.

interfaces.name: SystemClock
interfaces.signature: struct SystemClock: ClockProviding { func now() -> Date; func uptime() -> TimeInterval }
interfaces.notes: Production implementation. Tests supply a fake ClockProviding instead.

interfaces.name: LiveRunEngine
interfaces.signature: enum LiveRunEngine { static func project(event: Event, log: EventLog, clock: ClockProviding) -> LiveRunProjection }
interfaces.notes: Pure, static, no side effects. undoLastAction skips the immediately-preceding entry during replay, never truncates the log. Tested with Swift Testing @Test(arguments:), not XCTest.

interfaces.name: EventStatus
interfaces.signature: enum EventStatus: String, Codable, Sendable { case draft, ready, live, onHold, completed }
interfaces.notes: Derived for display from LiveRunProjection + cue count, never stored as an independent flag.

interfaces.name: EventManifestEntry
interfaces.signature: struct EventManifestEntry: Codable, Sendable { let id: UUID; var title: String; var status: EventStatus; var cueCount: Int; var finalDriftSummary: String? }
interfaces.notes: finalDriftSummary populated only for .completed rows, shown as muted text on Home.

interfaces.name: EventManifest
interfaces.signature: struct EventManifest: Codable, Sendable { var activeLiveEventID: UUID?; var entries: [EventManifestEntry] }
interfaces.notes: activeLiveEventID is a hint only — cold-launch routing must load that event's log and reconfirm isLive via LiveRunEngine.project before trusting it.

interfaces.name: EventStoring
interfaces.signature: protocol EventStoring: Sendable { func loadManifest() async throws -> EventManifest; func loadEvent(id: UUID) async throws -> Event; func saveEvent(_ event: Event) async throws; func loadLog(eventID: UUID) async throws -> EventLog; func appendLogEntry(_ entry: ActionLogEntry, eventID: UUID) async throws; func deleteEvent(id: UUID) async throws; func resolveActiveLiveEvent() async -> UUID? }
interfaces.notes: This protocol boundary IS the answer to the portfolio's 'cloud-ready without rewriting' requirement: a future CloudKit-backed store is a second conformance, zero call-site changes. EventStore is the only production conformance today.

interfaces.name: EventStore
interfaces.signature: actor EventStore: EventStoring { init(containerURL: URL) }
interfaces.notes: Lives in app target's Persistence/ folder, not a separate package. Backs onto App Group container. All writes are temp-file-then-atomic-rename. resolveActiveLiveEvent() re-projects the log before trusting the manifest pointer.

interfaces.name: PersistenceError
interfaces.signature: enum PersistenceError: Error, Sendable { case corruptEvent(UUID); case corruptManifest; case notFound(UUID); case writeFailed(underlying: String) }
interfaces.notes: corruptEvent quarantines one Home row; corruptManifest triggers the app-root persistence-error phase, a strictly worse case.

interfaces.name: AppGroupContainer
interfaces.signature: enum AppGroupContainer { static let identifier: String; static var containerURL: URL? }
interfaces.notes: Single source of the App Group id used by EventStore and any future widget/Live Activity extension.

interfaces.name: EntitlementResult
interfaces.signature: enum EntitlementResult: Sendable, Equatable { case entitled; case free; case unverifiable }
interfaces.notes: unverifiable is treated identically to free everywhere — fail open, no separate error UI.

interfaces.name: EntitlementService
interfaces.signature: actor EntitlementService { func checkEntitlement() async -> EntitlementResult }
interfaces.notes: Lives in its own local Swift Package, CueKeeperEntitlements — the ONE package boundary in this project, because it's the one place a forbidden import actually matters. Only app target's UI/CreationFlow/ files link this package; UI/LiveRun/ and LiveRunEngine cannot import it — a compile error, not a convention. Races Transaction.currentEntitlements against a 2s Task.sleep timeout; caches via Transaction.updates at launch/foreground.

interfaces.name: NotificationScheduler
interfaces.signature: struct NotificationScheduler { func rescheduleAll(for event: Event, log: EventLog, clock: ClockProviding) async }
interfaces.notes: App target's Notifications/ folder. Full cancel-and-reschedule of every remaining request on every mutating action, keyed by eventID+cueID. .timeSensitive interruption level.

interfaces.name: HapticPattern
interfaces.signature: enum HapticPattern: Sendable { case advance, hold, skip, contingencyReveal }
interfaces.notes: Maps to four distinguishable feedback-generator patterns, verified distinct on physical device.

interfaces.name: HapticsService
interfaces.signature: struct HapticsService { func play(_ pattern: HapticPattern) }
interfaces.notes: App target's Haptics/ folder. Thin wrapper over UIKit feedback generators.

interfaces.name: ClockJumpDetector
interfaces.signature: enum ClockJumpDetector { static func isAnomalous(previousWall: Date, previousUptime: TimeInterval, currentWall: Date, currentUptime: TimeInterval, tolerance: TimeInterval) -> Bool }
interfaces.notes: Pure function; feeds LiveRunProjection.clockJumpWarning. Called on every foreground/appear. Tested with Swift Testing.

interfaces.name: AppPhase
interfaces.signature: enum AppPhase: Equatable { case loadingPersistence; case console(UUID); case home }
interfaces.notes: Root switch, not a NavigationStack. WindowGroup renders directly off this.

interfaces.name: AppCoordinator
interfaces.signature: @Observable @MainActor final class AppCoordinator { var phase: AppPhase; func bootstrap() async; func startLiveRun(eventID: UUID) async; func returnToHome() }
interfaces.notes: bootstrap() reads the manifest, resolves the active event via EventStore.resolveActiveLiveEvent(), and only then sets phase — never renders Home first and redirects.

interfaces.name: LiveConsoleViewModel
interfaces.signature: @Observable @MainActor final class LiveConsoleViewModel { var projection: LiveRunProjection; var isContingencyRevealed: Bool; var undoBannerVisible: Bool; func advance() async; func hold() async; func skip() async; func jumpToBackup() async; func undo() async; func revealContingency() }
interfaces.notes: Lives in UI/LiveRun/. Body-relevant state is exactly `projection` plus transient UI flags. Cannot import CueKeeperEntitlements — that package isn't linked from UI/LiveRun/, so it's a build error, not a review catch.

interfaces.name: seedSampleEventForUITesting
interfaces.signature: func seedSampleEventForUITesting(store: EventStoring) async
interfaces.notes: Invoked when launch argument -uiTestSeedSampleEvent is present; drops app directly into a known live-console state for XCUITest tap-count assertions.

interfaces.name: AccessibilityAnnouncer
interfaces.signature: enum AccessibilityAnnouncer { static func announce(_ message: String) }
interfaces.notes: Wraps UIAccessibility.post(.announcement:); used for undo-banner appearance and clock-jump warnings.

interfaces.name: SnapshotPrivacyGuard
interfaces.signature: struct SnapshotPrivacyGuard: ViewModifier { func body(content: Content) -> some View }
interfaces.notes: Should-have blur-on-background for the live console; first cut under time pressure, must be named explicitly in the final report if cut.

interfaces.name: TemplateExporter
interfaces.signature: struct TemplateExporter { func export(_ event: Event, includeNotes: Bool) -> Data }
interfaces.notes: Should-have. Strips contingencyNote and role free text by default; includeNotes is an explicit opt-in.
